categories.title: Air & Atmosphere
categories.description: Real-time and historical air quality, pollutant concentrations, and atmospheric composition from U.S. and global monitoring networks.

datasets.name: AirNow — Current AQI
datasets.description: Real-time Air Quality Index by reporting area across the U.S. Covers O₃, PM2.5, PM10, CO, SO₂, and NO₂. Used in Local Reports Block 1.

datasets.name: EPA AirData / AQS — Annual Trends
datasets.description: Annual AQI, PM2.5, and ozone trends at county and CBSA level. Used for Local Reports Block 1 trend charts.

datasets.name: OpenAQ v3 — Global Air Monitors
datasets.description: Aggregated PM2.5 readings from thousands of ground-level sensors worldwide. Used for the Earth Now globe Air Monitors overlay.

datasets.name: CAMS — Smoke & Atmospheric Composition
datasets.description: Model-based aerosol optical depth, smoke plumes, and atmospheric composition. Requires a Copernicus ADS account. Globe Smoke layer is planned for P1.

categories.title: Water Quality, Drinking Water & Wastewater
categories.description: Discrete water-quality sampling results, drinking water compliance records, and wastewater facility data.

datasets.name: Water Quality Portal (WQP) — Discrete Samples
datasets.description: Aggregated portal for USGS NWIS, EPA STORET, and USDA water-quality samples. Uses WQX 3.0 beta endpoints. Used in Local Reports Block 4.

datasets.name: SDWIS — Safe Drinking Water Information System
datasets.description: Compliance and violation records for public water systems. Tracks health-based violations, monitoring failures, and enforcement actions. Served via Envirofacts REST at data.epa.gov/efservice/. Mandatory disclaimer: regulatory violations ≠ unsafe water at the tap.

categories.title: Hydrology & Floods
categories.description: Near-real-time streamflow, stage height, and flood forecasts from stream gauges and forecast models.

datasets.name: USGS Modernized Water Data — Streamflow (NRT)
datasets.description: Continuous streamflow and stage height from thousands of stream gauges via the modernized OGC API Features. Legacy WaterServices endpoint decommissions Q1 2027. Used in Local Reports Block 4.

datasets.name: NOAA River Forecast Centers — Flood Predictions
datasets.description: Short- and long-range river stage forecasts from 13 Regional River Forecast Centers. Includes flood category thresholds (minor, moderate, major).

categories.title: Coast & Ocean
categories.description: Sea surface temperature, tides, ocean buoy observations, and marine ecosystem indicators.

datasets.name: NOAA OISST — Daily Sea Surface Temperature
datasets.description: Optimally-interpolated sea surface temperature on a 0.25° global grid. Served via NOAA CoastWatch ERDDAP (ncdcOisst21NrtAgg). Used for the Earth Now Ocean Heat overlay.

datasets.name: NOAA CO-OPS — Tides & Water Levels
datasets.source: NOAA Center for Operational Oceanographic Products and Services
datasets.description: Tidal predictions, observed water levels, and storm surge data from the U.S. coastal gauge network.

datasets.name: NOAA NDBC — Ocean Buoy Network
datasets.description: Wave height, period, wind speed, and sea surface temperature from hundreds of moored and drifting buoys.

categories.title: Soil, Land & Site Condition
categories.description: Global soil property maps, national land cover, and contaminated site inventories.

datasets.name: EPA SEMS / Superfund NPL — Contaminated Sites
datasets.source: US EPA Superfund Enterprise Management System
datasets.description: Active and archived Superfund sites on the National Priorities List (NPL), including site boundaries, NPL status, and contaminants of concern. Served via the EPA FAC_Superfund_Site_Boundaries ArcGIS FeatureServer — polygon centroids computed client-side for map placement.

datasets.name: EPA ACRES — Brownfields Properties
datasets.description: Grantee-reported Brownfields sites (formerly industrial/commercial properties with real or perceived contamination that complicate redevelopment). Served via the EPA EMEF/efpoints ArcGIS MapServer Layer 5. Cleanup status lives on a separate ACRES endpoint and is not yet joined.

datasets.name: SoilGrids — Global Soil Properties
datasets.description: 250m global maps of soil organic carbon, pH, texture, bulk density, and other properties, derived from machine-learning models trained on field profiles.

datasets.name: NLCD — National Land Cover Database
datasets.description: 30m Landsat-derived classification of U.S. land cover into 20 classes: forests, wetlands, developed, agriculture, open water, etc.

categories.title: Waste & Materials
categories.description: Hazardous waste generation, toxics releases, and material flow tracking at facility and national levels.

datasets.name: EPA TRI — Toxics Release Inventory
datasets.description: Annual facility-level reports of releases, transfers, and disposal of ~800 listed toxic chemicals to air, water, and land. Publicly available back to 1987. Served via Envirofacts REST (tri_facility + tri_reporting_form) at data.epa.gov/efservice/. Self-reported — facilities below reporting thresholds are invisible.

datasets.name: EPA RCRAInfo — Hazardous Waste Tracking
datasets.description: Generator, transporter, and treatment/storage/disposal facility data under RCRA. Tracks waste quantities, compliance status, and permit information.

categories.title: Emissions, Energy & Facilities
categories.description: Regulated facility inventory, compliance history, and greenhouse gas emissions from stationary sources.

datasets.name: EPA ECHO — Facility Compliance & Enforcement
datasets.source: US EPA Enforcement and Compliance History Online
datasets.description: Regulatory compliance status, violation history, and enforcement actions for air (CAA), water (CWA), and hazardous waste (RCRA) programs. Used in Local Reports Block 3.

datasets.name: Climate TRACE — GHG Emissions by Facility
datasets.description: Independent, satellite-derived greenhouse gas emission estimates for 352M+ individual sources — power plants, shipping, agriculture, and more.

datasets.name: EPA GHGRP (FLIGHT) — Facility GHG Emissions
datasets.source: US EPA Greenhouse Gas Reporting Program
datasets.description: Self-reported facility-level greenhouse gas emissions under 40 CFR Part 98, covering facilities emitting ≥25,000 tCO₂e/yr. Surfaced via the Envirofacts GHG REST service (pub_dim_facility + pub_facts_sector_ghg_emission). Complements Climate TRACE estimated emissions for U.S. facilities.

datasets.name: EPA eGRID — Power Plant Emissions
datasets.description: Comprehensive emission and resource data for U.S. electricity generation. Covers CO₂, SO₂, NOₓ, and heat input at the plant, boiler, and grid subregion level.
datasets.spatial_coverage: U.S. power plants and grid regions

categories.title: Climate, Hazards & Exposure
categories.description: Long-term climate signals, extreme weather hazards, and community exposure indices.

datasets.name: NASA FIRMS — Active Wildfires (NRT)
datasets.source: NASA Fire Information for Resource Management System
datasets.description: VIIRS and MODIS thermal anomaly detections at 375m resolution. Fire Radiative Power (FRP) indicates fire intensity. Used for the Earth Now Fires overlay.

datasets.name: NOAA GML Mauna Loa — CO₂ Concentration
datasets.description: Continuous atmospheric CO₂ measurements from the Mauna Loa Observatory (1958–present). The definitive long-term record of greenhouse gas accumulation.

datasets.name: NOAAGlobalTemp CDR — Temperature Anomaly
datasets.source: NOAA National Centers for Environmental Information
datasets.description: Global surface temperature anomaly relative to the 1991–2020 climatology. Derived from the NOAAGlobalTemp v6.1 combined land-ocean product (1850–present).

datasets.name: NSIDC Sea Ice Index — Arctic Extent
datasets.source: National Snow and Ice Data Center
datasets.description: Daily Arctic and Antarctic sea ice extent from passive microwave satellite data (1979–present). G02135 v4.0 provides a consistent long-term record.

datasets.name: FEMA NRI — National Risk Index
datasets.description: County-level composite risk scores for 18 natural hazard types, incorporating expected annual loss, social vulnerability, and community resilience.
